next_action: Move to CP7 peer outbound download session.

current_evidence: Outgoing peer TCP, hello, eMule info, file request/status, hashset, accept upload, queue rank, normal parts, compressed parts, and part hash verification are implemented for the current outbound download path. Peer queue, part-status, connecting, accepted, requested-part ranges, out-of-parts, cancelled, no-file, dead-peer, failure, and reconnect-backoff state has first-class PeerState ownership. Peer scheduling skips dead peers until retry time and skips already connecting peers. Peer TCP OP_QUEUERANK/OP_QUEUERANKING updates PeerState queue rank, peer command connection failures enter PeerState retry backoff, corrupt full-piece hash failures have local socket coverage proving PeerState retry backoff, successful/cancelled/failed peer lifecycles clear requested-part state, EOF during partial packet reads enters PeerState retry backoff instead of silently rescheduling, and unexpected peer part ranges are rejected before disk writes. Peer data write, SegmentMan completion, and MD4 verification live in Ed2kPeerTransfer instead of the overloaded command. Normal part, compressed part, and 64-bit request payload behavior now have focused evidence. Incoming peer acceptance remains CP8. Source Exchange merge policy remains CP9. Full ED2K-aware piece policy remains CP10. Upload responses remain CP14.
acceptance_criteria: Peer state machine handles 32-bit and 64-bit requests, normal and compressed parts, out-of-parts, cancel transfer, duplicate rejection, failure scoring, reconnect backoff, and corrupt piece retry.
verification: cmake --build --preset default --target aria2_tests; ctest --preset default --output-on-failure -R aria2_tests; git diff --check
next_action: Move to CP8 incoming peer listener.

current_evidence: Incoming ED2K TCP connections are accepted through Ed2kListenCommand on aria2-next's existing EventPoll path. The listener is created once per engine from ED2K request-group startup, honors --ed2k-listen-port, routes only when there is a unique active ED2K download group, rejects duplicate active endpoints, and hands accepted sockets to Ed2kCommand. Incoming peer Hello packets now normalize the temporary TCP source endpoint to the advertised ED2K listen port and user hash before continuing through the peer state machine. Shared/completed-file matching remains CP13/CP14 ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Incoming ED2K peer connections for active downloads are accepted through aria2-next's event loop, deduplicated, and routed into the peer state machine. Shared/completed file upload matching is intentionally deferred to the shared store and upload checkpoints.
verification: git diff --check; cmake --build --preset default --target aria2_tests; ctest --preset default --output-on-failure -R aria2_tests
